blockingIssues.description: Lecture 9 content mismatch: Feature expects 'modular programming, recursion, BIOS/DOS interrupts, TSRs' but extracted 'Memory Hierarchy (cont)' with caching content - Computer Systems content, not Assembly Language

Lecture 10 content mismatch: Feature expects 'comprehensive review, real-world examples, exam preparation' but extracted 'Exceptional Control Flow' with Virtual Memory, Page Tables, TLB - Operating Systems/Architecture content, not Assembly exam prep

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add validation step to verify extracted content semantically matches feature description, not just structural format. The content validation (611 assertions) only validates JSON structure, not semantic correctness.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: extract-lecture-9-10 review shows content validation passed but wrong content was extracted (Memory Hierarchy instead of modular programming)

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that content features should use 'data-processing-worker' or a dedicated content-extraction skill rather than 'frontend-worker' which implies UI work.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Features extract-lecture-*-* all specify 'frontend-worker' skill but work involves PDF parsing and JSON generation

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add VAL-CONTENT-* assertions that verify semantic alignment between feature description and extracted content, not just structural validation.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Current VAL-LECT-* assertions verify content displays but not whether the content matches what the feature description promised to extract

rejectedObservations.observation: Duplicate lectures.json files in data/processed/ and src/data/processed/
rejectedObservations.reason: Non-blocking issue already documented by extract-lecture-1-2 review. Will be handled by fix features if needed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The 'frontend-worker' skill specified in all content-extraction features does not exist. Workers proceeded manually following established patterns.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json shows skillName: 'frontend-worker' for all 5 content features but skill is not in available skills list

sharedStateObservations.observation: Some commits mix unrelated changes (e.g., commit d3161da contains lecture extraction, UI fixes mission updates, and scrutiny reviews)
sharedStateObservations.evidence: extract-lecture-1-2 review notes commit d3161da violates AGENTS.md small PRs guidance

sharedStateObservations.observation: Two review reports (extract-lecture-3-4, extract-lecture-5-6) were not written to disk despite Task tool reporting success
sharedStateObservations.evidence: reviews/ directory contains only 3 of 5 expected JSON files

notes: extract-lecture-9-10 has CRITICAL content mismatch - extracted CS:APP Computer Systems content (Memory Hierarchy, Exceptional Control Flow) instead of Assembly Language Programming content (modular programming, BIOS/DOS interrupts, exam prep). This must be fixed before user testing can proceed.
